description: 9:16 magazine-spread reel. Hard horizontal split (top editorial card / bottom naturally-graded speaker), classical display serif, italic-serif single-word captions, numbered rows with one-row color-break flagging the exception, slow 2.5-4.0s dwell per spread, crossfade or black-bezel-breath transitions. Print-quarterly register: paper-cream pages, hairline rules, tracked-out small-caps mastheads, no whips, no chrome, no neon.
fits_script_when: script reads as an essay, framework explanation, or curated breakdown rather than a hot-take or a comedy reel
speaker is on a locked-down podcast/desk/kitchen-counter plate (NOT walking-and-talking, NOT pure voiceover)
topic benefits from chapter-style framing — multiple 'pieces' or 'sections' that each get their own dwell beat
tone is considered, slower-paced, instructive — viewer is meant to read on-screen text, not just hear narration
script enumerates a list of N items where ONE item is the exception, the winner, or the answer the rest sets up
total duration is 50-90 seconds (the dwell math doesn't support shorter reels)
the speaker's environment (real kitchen, plants, a Røde mic, hoodie, framed art) is part of the credibility
ends with a low-key italic-serif comment-keyword CTA, not a hype call-to-action
does_not_fit_when: speaker is walking-and-talking outdoors or in motion (split format requires a locked plate)
script is comedy, reaction, or hype-energy (the 2.5-4.0s dwell breaks the format)
video is under 30 seconds (dwell math collapses)
script is a tool-by-tool list of SaaS products with logos and concept demos (use sage-stack instead)
live screen recording of an actual app is essential to the explanation (use screen-face-split)
ad-style headline + product surface required (use screen-face-split)
topic demands kinetic, whip-cut, B-roll-heavy energy (any whip transition breaks the editorial register)

patterns.name: Issue masthead opening spread
patterns.description: Opening 2-4s spread that frames the reel as a magazine article. Cream card slides in from the top of the bezel with a tracked-out small-caps masthead ('ISSUE 01 · THE HARNESS · APR 2026'), thin black hairline rule below, then a serif headline pair lands underneath. Establishes 'you are reading something curated' before any narration content begins.

trigger.script_signal: the opening line of the script frames the topic as a piece of considered writing — declarative noun phrase, often introducing a theme ('the agent harness', 'context engineering', 'five things around a model') that the rest of the reel will unpack

issue_topic.description: Middle masthead segment naming the theme (e.g. 'THE HARNESS', 'THE LOOP')

issue_date.description: Right masthead segment with month + year (e.g. 'APR 2026')

headline_upright.description: Voice A — structural words of the headline pair (e.g. 'Five')

headline_italic.description: Voice B — italic emphasis word inside the headline (e.g. 'things')

headline_tail.description: Optional trailing words after the italic (e.g. 'around a model')

examples.note: Masthead 'ISSUE 03 · THE LOOP · APR 2026' slides in from top, hairline rule, then headline pair builds underneath
Headline pair completes — 'Five things around a model' with italic 'things'

patterns.name: Numbered rows with one row breaking the underline color
patterns.description: THE signature pattern. A cream card holds 3-5 rows, each with [italic numeral] [serif word] [tracked-out grey subtitle] [thin colored underline]. All underlines land in --primary-mid first; then ONE row crossfades to --accent-warm and gets a small '↳ THE EXCEPTION' (or 'THE ANSWER' / 'THE WINNER') tag beside it. The exception swap is timed to the speaker's 'but', 'except', 'unless' or equivalent.

trigger.script_signal: speaker enumerates a list of N items (N=3-5) where one item is positioned as the exception, the winner, the answer, or the punchline that the rest sets up. Often phrased as 'the X things... but the real one is Y' or 'X options... only one of them actually Z'

rows.description: JSON-stringified array of row objects: [{num:'01', word:'Loop', sub:'one instruction at a time'}, ...]. 3-5 rows.

exception_index.description: Zero-based index of the row whose underline swaps to --accent-warm. Usually the last or second-to-last row.

exception_tag.description: Small-caps grey tag that appears beside the broken row (e.g. 'THE EXCEPTION', 'THE ANSWER', 'THE WINNER')

exception_swap_offset_s.description: Seconds after pattern start when the exception row's underline crossfades. Time this to the speaker's 'but'/'except'.

examples.note: 5 rows (Loop / Tools / Memory / Context / Control) cascade with lime underlines
Row 04 underline crossfades from lime to orange, '↳ THE EXCEPTION' tag enters from left

patterns.name: Two-voice serif section header with hairline rule
patterns.description: Marks the start of a new piece/section inside the magazine. Tracked-out small-caps masthead label ('PIECE 01 · THE LOOP', '↳ GO DEEPER') appears, hairline rule scales in below it, then a big serif headline pair lands — Voice A upright + Voice B italic emphasis word — with another hairline rule beneath. ~1.5-2.0s total.

trigger.script_signal: speaker transitions into a new section, often with phrasing like 'piece two', 'next is X', 'the second thing is', 'first, the X', 'and then there's', or an explicit chapter-style cue

masthead_text.description: Tracked-out small-caps label above the headline (e.g. 'PIECE 01 · THE LOOP', '↳ GO DEEPER')

headline_italic.description: Voice B italic emphasis word/phrase (e.g. 'instruction', 'agent harness')

headline_tail.description: Optional trailing upright words (e.g. 'at a time')

examples.note: 'PIECE 01 · THE LOOP' masthead, hairline, big serif 'Loop' word with subtitle
'↳ GO DEEPER' masthead opens the closing recap section

patterns.name: Hero editorial pull-quote
patterns.description: Massive italic serif pull-quote (~64-88px) occupies the top half cream card with a thin hairline rule above and below. Attribution appears in tracked-out small-caps grey beneath. Reads as 'a magazine quote loaded onto the page' — fades in as one element, no per-word stagger. Held with breath. Lands once per reel as the rhetorical landing.

trigger.script_signal: speaker delivers a single quotable sentence that summarizes the thesis or names a sharp distinction — short, declarative, no list, no qualification ('it's not a tool, it's a teammate', 'the loop is the product')

quote_text.description: The quoted sentence in italic serif. Keep under ~14 words.

attribution.description: Optional small-caps grey attribution line below

use_flash_landing.description: If true, precede the quote with a 0.25s flash-through-white. Use sparingly, max 1x per reel.

examples.note: 'It's not a tool. It's a teammate.' italic pull-quote with small-caps attribution beneath

patterns.name: Geist Mono code block on cream card
patterns.description: Light card-within-card sitting on the cream page. Geist Mono (or Roboto Mono / JetBrains Mono) at ~16-22px, dark grey text on a slightly cooler off-white panel (~#f8f6ee). No syntax highlighting beyond grey — purposely undecorated, reads as 'a snippet from the editor, dropped onto the page'.

trigger.script_signal: speaker references actual code, a config snippet, a terminal command, or JSON output — the on-page artifact is text the viewer is meant to read literally

code_text.description: The literal text content rendered in monospace. Preserve indentation. Keep under ~8 lines.

language_label.description: Optional small-caps grey label above the block (e.g. 'TS', 'JSON', 'BASH')

panel_tint.description: Background of the code panel: 'warm-off-white' | 'cream-shade' (slightly darker than the page)

examples.note: Geist Mono code block on cream card, no syntax highlighting, dark grey monospace

patterns.name: Fake-UI window inside cream card
patterns.description: A light-mode software-window mockup sits on the cream page — chrome dots, optional title bar, content area styled as a chat, list, dashboard, form, or app surface. Soft 16px drop shadow, slight rounded corners (~14px), occasional 4-8deg tilt for 3D presence.

trigger.script_signal: speaker references a specific UI surface — an app screenshot, a Notion template, a chat window, a form, a dashboard — and the on-page content needs to look like that surface even though no live recording exists

content.description: The literal text/structure to render inside the UI. Format depends on ui_type.

tilt_deg.description: Optional rotateY tilt for 3D presence (0-8deg). Default 0 = flat on page.

tint_swatch_token.description: Optional accent inside the UI (button color, highlight bar) — must reference a brand token

examples.note: Light Cursor / Claude Code window screenshot floats on cream page, 16px drop shadow
Cream form mockup with topic field, radio chips, slider, swatch palette at bottom
Notion templates rendered as light-card UI inside the editorial spread

patterns.name: Speaker visible with italic-serif single-word caption
patterns.description: The bottom-half speaker plate runs continuously through every spread. This pattern names the per-beat caption layer that floats over the speaker's torso ~1/3 from top of the bottom half: a single italic-serif white word, ~52-64px, with soft 6-8px black drop shadow, no pill background, no all-caps.

trigger.script_signal: speaker hits an emphatic word inside a sentence — typically the noun or verb that carries the meaning of the beat, often the same word that appears on the top-half page (meta-moment alignment)

word_text.description: The single italic-serif word to display. Keep to one word (occasionally two if grammatically tight).

emphasis_token.description: Optional brand color for the word. Default white; swap to --primary-mid to match a top-half emphasis.

vertical_position.description: Where the caption sits in the bottom half: 'third-from-top' | 'centered' | 'lower-third'

examples.note: italic 'different' lands as the exception row underline crossfades to orange
italic 'that' over speaker as flowchart diagram is held above

patterns.name: Crossfade or black-bezel-breath spread transition
patterns.description: How the format moves between two editorial spreads. Two flavors: (1) plain GSAP opacity crossfade ~0.4s power2.inOut between two cream cards while the bottom-half speaker plays continuously; (2) black-bezel breath — the cream card fades out over the bezel, the top half holds pure black for 200-400ms, then the next card slides in from the top with expo.out. Never a whip, never a directional blur, never a hard cut between two cream cards.

trigger.script_signal: transition between two spreads — the speaker continues talking but the editorial top half needs to swap. Used between every piece of the magazine.

transition_type.description: 'crossfade' (0.4s opacity, default 70% of cases) | 'bezel-breath' (fade out + 200-400ms black hold + slide-in)

breath_duration_s.description: If transition_type=bezel-breath, how long the black hold is (0.2-0.4s)

ease.description: GSAP easing. 'power2.inOut' for crossfades, 'expo.out' for slide-ins. Never bounce/elastic/back.

examples.note: Crossfades between piece spreads — bottom-half speaker continuous
Black-bezel breath ~300ms before the next cream card slides in from top

patterns.name: Two-voice serif comment-keyword closer
patterns.description: Final 2-3s of the reel. Cream card centered with a thin hairline rule above and below: Voice A upright serif 'comment' + Voice B italic-serif quoted keyword (e.g. 'the loop'). Speaker may smile under the bezel. Held to the end. Same shape as sage-stack's comment-CTA but rendered in editorial-magazine's paper-page register.

trigger.script_signal: speaker explicitly asks viewer to comment a keyword to receive something (a doc, a guide, a DM, the script, the template)

verb_text.description: Voice A action verb in upright serif (e.g. 'comment', 'reply')

keyword_text.description: Voice B italic-serif keyword in quotes (e.g. 'the loop', 'harness', 'stack')

show_quotes.description: Whether to wrap keyword_text in curly serif quotes

examples.note: Cream card: 'comment' upright + italic 'the loop' centered, hairline above and below

patterns.name: Italic-serif single-word caption (default fallback)
patterns.description: When no other pattern matches a beat, this is the safety net. Single italic-serif white word over the speaker's torso — same visual as speaker-with-italic-caption but no top-half editorial spread above. Used for connector beats, simple claims, transitions without dedicated patterns.

trigger.script_signal: fallback when no other pattern scores above 0.5

emphasis_words.description: Comma-separated list of words to highlight in --primary-mid (rest stay white). Default: no recoloring, all white italic.

examples.note: Full-frame talking head with italic-serif single-word captions throughout — the format reduced to its caption layer
Continuous caption layer running under all editorial spreads

open_questions: Should fake-ui-mock be split into separate patterns per ui_type? Each has different layout needs and different trigger language. Defer to v0.2 once you have more example reels.
The 'flowchart diagram' beat only appears once. Could become its own pattern (hand-drawn-diagram) but needs a second example.
Reel A uses white-on-black-pill all-caps captions for HOOK lines on the full-frame talking-head variant. Could be its own hook-pill-caption pattern.
The big-stat card beat (single large numeral mid-page) is structurally similar to hero-pull-quote but renders a number/stat. Could be its own stat-card pattern. For v0.1, fold into hero-pull-quote.
Light-leak warm-tint transition is a sub-variant of crossfade-or-bezel-breath-transition.
crossfade-or-bezel-breath-transition is technically two transitions sharing a pattern. Watch for whether the planner can pick the right transition_type from script signal alone.
